body.p: The Gehtsoft Entity Framework Library provides the following features:
1) Simplified and cross-platform basic ADO.NET query.

2) Cross-DB server query constructors for [c]CREATE TABLE[/c], [c]DROP TABLE[/c], [c]CREATE VIEW[/c], [c]DROP VIEW[/c], [c]CREATE INDEX[/c], [c]DROP INDEX[/c], [c]INSERT[/c], [c]UPDATE[/c], [c]DELETE[/c] and [c]SELECT[/c] queries.
3) Entity-level CRUD operations.
The following Databases are supported:
1) Microsoft SQL Server 2002+
2) Oracle 11+
3) MySQL 5.5+
4) Postgres 8+
5) SQLite 3+
Why Gehtsoft.EF may be better than the standard EF framework for some projects?
There are a few reasons when Gehtsoft.EF framework would be more effective:

The framework is 100% pure code-first framework and does NOT require any kind of dependency between the project and the certain database instance. This helps to run the project in distributed environment and even let different developers use different database (not instances, but literally database engines, e.g. Postgres and SQLite) while working on the same project. This also lets use SQLite for running unittests instead of mocking DAO behavior that saves time to develop unit tests and improves the coverage.

The framework is 100% database-agnostic and domain-driven-design focused. Create as many context as you need, mix and match them. Use the database driver that matches you needs the best - from in-memory SQLite for automated tests to Oracle for production. And let the framework to take care of all the nuances.

While framework supports LINQ, it is heavily oriented to be used without LINQ. The reason is that LINQ is not 100% compatible with SQL select query structure and therefore the LINQ queries compiled into SQL SELECT queries are far from optimal. Gehtsoft.EF framework generates selects which are almost as good as written manually. Unfortunately, due to reason above, LINQ-produced queries are as bad as for any other LINQ-based framework.
It is lighter and do not consists of needless dependencies. Actually, for the most queries it is as fast as it would be running these queries directly to the database without EF at all.
It allows to use plain and raw SQL. Moreover, it allows to use SQL constructors which creates database-specific SQL SELECTs on the fly.
Last but not not least - it is open source. Understand how it works, make it better, make it custom!
Clone and fork EF report at [eurl=https://github.com/gehtsoft-usa/gehtsoft.ef]Gehtsoft Entity Framework[/eurl]
This section of the documentation demonstrates some basic principles of using database framework.
Please not that tutorials do not cover every topic and nuance and are intended to introduce the reader into the library.
Careful reading of the API documentation is advised.
This section of the documentation describes how to use the framework with databases directly using SQL queries.

classDiagram class SqlDbConnection { } <<abstract>> SqlDbConnection
class SqlDbQuery class SqlDbTransaction
SqlDbConnection ..> SqlDbQuery : creates SqlDbConnection ..> SqlDbTransaction : creates
class Attribute class EntityAttribute class EntityPropertyAttribute
EntityAttribute --|> Attribute EntityPropertyAttribute --|> Attribute
EntityAttribute ..> MyClass : defines metadata EntityPropertyAttribute ..> MyClass : defines metadata class MyClass
In Mongo DB many lists contains a complex tree of objects. The way to refer an element of this tree is called "Path".
The path consists of elements separater by a dot character ([c].[/c]).
Each element could be:
A name of the property
An integer zero-based index of an array element

E.g. path "[c]P1.P2.PA.5.CC[/c]" means: the property [c]CC[/c] of sixth element of the array stored in the property [c]PA[/c] of an object stored in the property [c]P2[/c] of the object stored in the property [c]P1[/c] of the entity.
In case the path is used in the condition and condition needs to be applied to any element of the array, just skip index in the path.

group.title: Database Connectivity
group.brief: This section explains how to create a connection and use it with plain SQL

group.title: Entity Operations
group.brief: This section explains how to the framework to manage the persistent storage of the entities.

body.p: The framework uses [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.SqlDbConnection]SqlDbConnection[/clink] class to perform all connection-related operations.

To get an instance of the connection, the developer must use one of the database factory classes which are named as [c][i]DbName[/i]DbConnectionFactory[/c] (e.g) [c][i]Mssql[/i]ConnectionFactory[/c] and always has a method named [c]Create[/c] which always has a following prototype:
[c]SqlDbConnection [b]Create[/b](string connectionString)[/c]
or for asynchronous version
[c]Task<SqlDbConnection> [b]CreateAsync[/b](string connectionString, CancellationToken?)[/c]

The delegate definitions are [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.SqlDbConnectionFactory]SqlDbConnectionFactory[/clink] and [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.SqlDbConnectionFactoryAsync]SqlDbConnectionFactoryAsync[/clink]
Use this method in order to get an instance of the connection.
Dispose the connection object when it is not in use.
You can also use [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.UniversalSqlDbFactory]UniversalSqlDbFactory[/clink] to create a connection by the driver name.
[b]DB-specific Implementations[/b]

Wherever the application needs to know any platform specific, an instance of [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.SqlDbLanguageSpecifics]SqlDbLanguageSpecifics[/clink] may be obtained from the connection object via [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.SqlDbConnection.GetLanguageSpecifics.3e5]GetLanguageSpecifics()[/clink] method.
[b]Pooling[/b]

The framework is build over ADO.NET technology so it has a connection pooling embed. All connections that shares the same connection string aren't actually closed at disposition, but are returned back to the connection pool for further reuse.
So, it is recommended to limit lifetime of the connection object to quite a short period of time, e.g. one request handling, and dispose it as soon as it reasonably possible.

There is only once case when the behavior isn't clear and well-defined, it is in-memory SQLite database. In this case is it recommended to keep one instance of the connection during the whole lifecycle of the application.
[b]Example[/b]
The simplest way to work with the library is to use plain SQL queries.
To execute a query:
Get an instance of a query using [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.SqlDbConnection.GetQuery]GetQuery()[/clink] method.
Bind parameters (if any) using [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.SqlDbQuery.BindNull.5v7]BindNull()[/clink] and [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.SqlDbQuery.BindParam]BindParam()[/clink] methods.

Execute query using either [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.SqlDbQuery.ExecuteNoData.AiD]ExecuteNoData()[/clink] (for queries that do not produce resultset, e.g. [c]UPDATE[/c]) or [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.SqlDbQuery.ExecuteReader.5HC]ExecuteReader()[/clink] for [c]SELECT[/c] queries.

For the queries that has resultset, go trough the resultset using [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.SqlDbQuery.ReadNext.tUA]ReadNext()[/clink] method and read values using [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.SqlDbQuery.GetValue]GetValue()[/clink] method).
In this mode the library provides almost no service over the regular ADO.NET library.
To execute a few queries within a transaction using [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.SqlDbConnection.BeginTransaction]BeginTransaction()[/clink] of the connection.
When the transaction is finished, complete it using [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.SqlDbTransaction.Commit.BU8]Commit()[/clink] method.
If the transaction is finished not successfully, roll back it using [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.SqlDbTransaction.Rollback.8s6]Rollback()[/clink] method.

To check whether the database supports the transactions or whether it supports nested transactions use the method. [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.SqlDbLanguageSpecifics]SqlDbLanguageSpecifics[/clink].[clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.SqlDbLanguageSpecifics.SupportsTransactions.hbA]SupportsTransactions[/clink].
Please note that in case the database does not support nested transactions but it supports save points, the nested transaction will be simulated via save points.

article.title: Table Descriptors and Query Builder for CREATE TABLE

body.p: To make the application independent from the specifics of a particular SQL engine, the library offers the feature called "query builders". The query builder is a class that relies on table metadata and creates a RAW SQL that reflects specific of certain SQL engine. To start using this feature the developer must create metadata for all the tables used in the application. The metadata must be described via [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.QueryBuilder.TableDescriptor]TableDescriptor[/clink] class.

example.title: Example: Table Descriptor (these tables will be used in all examples in this section

body.p: Now you can create the table at any database using [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.QueryBuilder.CreateTableBuilder]CreateTableBuilder[/clink] class. This class will create a raw SQL that reflects all specifics related to implementation of [c]CREATE TABLE[/c] command and supported types/features of the SQL engine to which the application is connected.

example.title: Example: Creating the table using the table descriptor

body.p: using (SqlDbQuery query = connection.GetQuery(connection.GetCreateTableBuilder(my_table))) query.ExecuteNoData();
[b][c]INSERT[/c][/b]

The [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.QueryBuilder.InsertQueryBuilder]InsertQueryBuilder[/clink] creates the SQL engine specific query for the INSERT and defines all parameters. The parameters has example the same name as the columns to be inserted. Please note that the query builder takes care about proper using of suffixes for the parameters (e.g. [c]@[/c] for MSSQL or [c]:[/c] for Oracle) so wherever you are working with the SQL builder you can simply use the parameter name without any prefixes. It also handles autoincrement columns and defines output parameter that accepts auto-assigned value.
[b][c]DELETE[/c][/b]

The [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.QueryBuilder.DeleteQueryBuilder]DeleteQueryBuilder[/clink] creates the SQL engine specific query to delete rows from the table. In this example we look at the simplest version that just deletes one row by its identifier. Use [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.QueryBuilder.DeleteQueryBuilder.DeleteById.H26]DeleteById()[/clink] method to create such query. The parameter with the same name as a primary key column will be created.
[b][c]UPDATE[/c][/b]

The [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.QueryBuilder.UpdateQueryBuilder]UpdateQueryBuilder[/clink] create the SQL engine specific query to update rows in the table. In this example we look at the simplest version that just updates one row by its identifier. Use [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.QueryBuilder.UpdateQueryBuilder.UpdateById.hqC]UpdateById[/clink] method to create such query. Use [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.QueryBuilder.UpdateQueryBuilder.AddUpdateColumn.xv3]AddUpdateColumn[/clink] to add one column to update. Use [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.QueryBuilder.UpdateQueryBuilder.AddUpdateAllColumns.wlF]AddUpdateAllColumns[/clink] to add all columns except primary key to update. Both methods create parameter(s) with the same name as the column name to be updated.
[b]Add a simple filter[/b]

To add a simple filtering to the query use [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.QueryBuilder.QueryWithWhereBuilder.Where.4CB]QueryWithWhereBuilder.Where[/clink] property. To add the filter you need to specify:
A logical operation to join the filter to the previous filters ([clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Entities.LogOp]LogOp[/clink]: AND(default) or OR).
The column to be filtered
The comparison operation [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Entities.CmpOp]CmpOp[/clink] (e.g. equal, not equal, less, like and so on).
The name of the parameter, the reference to another column in query or a subquery to compare the column with.

example.title: Example: Deleting all rows that matches one or another condition

body.p: [b]Add a group of filters[/b]

Sometime, for better readability or to define strict precedence of the logical operations, a group is used. A group is logically equal to brackets in SQL query expressions. Use [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.QueryBuilder.ConditionBuilder.AddGroup.qJ3]AddGroup()[/clink] to start a group of filters. Dispose the object returned by this method to end the group.

example.title: Example: Deleting all rows that matches one or another condition

body.p: [b]Simple Select[/b]

To build a [c]SELECT[/c] query use [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.QueryBuilder.SelectQueryBuilder]SelectQueryBuilder[/clink]. You can specify resultset, filter and ordering using methods available in this class. Below is an example of a simple single-table select with simple filter and simple order.
[b]Joining tables[/b]

Use the method [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.QueryBuilder.SelectQueryBuilder.AddTable.7v7]AddTable[/clink] to add a table to the query. In the most cases the table to be added has foreign key relationship to one of the tables in the query, so you don't have to specify the exact join condition. However, in case the tables do not have exactly defined relationships, you can always disable automatic table connecting and condigure join via [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.QueryBuilder.QueryBuilderEntity]QueryBuilderEntity[/clink] or simply use [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.QueryBuilder.QueryWithWhereBuilder.Where.4CB]Where[/clink] of the query to define join condition SQL89-style.
[b]More Examples[/b]
There are a few more self-explaining examples of using SELECT query.

Sometime subqueries require references to the parent queries. In this case [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.QueryBuilder.QueryWithWhereBuilder.GetReference]GetReference()[/clink] method may be used to get a reference for further usage in [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.EntityQueries.ConditionEntityQueryBase.Where.ty7]Where[/clink] property.

example.title: Example: Using Subqueries with References Between Queries

body.p: [b]What is Entity?[/b]
In the entity framework an entity is supposed to be ... an entity, in other words a special kind of a class which keeps only data and does not have any methods.
[b]How to Define Entity?[/b]
The entity which is good for an entity framework must:
be declared as a public class
have parameterless constructor
have entity data declared as public properties with get and set accessors defined.
The entity is attributed using [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Entities.EntityAttribute]EntityAttribute[/clink]. The attribute sets the table name.
[b]How to Define Property?[/b]
The properties are attributed using [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Entities.EntityPropertyAttribute]EntityPropertyAttribute[/clink]. In the simplest case nothing additional needs to be set.
In this case the property name, type and parameters are all taken from the property definition.
[b]When To Configure Property Size?[/b]
For some types the property size cannot be get from the property definition (e.g. for strings) or may be not effective if it is taken from the property definition (e.g. numbers).
In this case [c]Size[/c] and [c]Precision[/c] (for numbers) attribute properties can be utilized.
[b]When To Set Property Type?[/b]
For some types the appropriate database type cannot be get from the property definition. For example, C# for not have special types for date and timestamps, but the most of database do.
In this case the DB type needs to be specified.
[b]How to Make a Property Nullable?[/b]
To make a property nullable, set [c]Nullable[/c] property of the attribute to [c]true[/c]. Also, if the property type is a value type, change the property type to nullable.
[b]How to Add an Index to the Property?[/b]
To add an index to the property use [c]Sorted[/c] property of the attribute.
[b]How to Customize Database Field Name?[/b]
If you want to customize the database field name use [c]Field[/c] property of the attribute.
The entity framework does not support complex primary keys. Only one property can be a primary key of the entity.
To mark the property as a primary key use [c]PrimaryKey[/c] property of the attribute. The property of any type except properties mapped to BLOB and CLOB columns can be declared as primary key.
When a property is declared as a primary key, it will be used for:
Updating the entity in the database
Loading a single instance of the entity from the database
Establishing relationship with other entities

For integer artificial primary keys the [c]Autoincrement[/c] property can be used to create an sequence that starts from 1 and increases by one every time when a new entity is saved to the database. This operation is atomic at the database level and uniqueness of the values of guaranteed.
A shortcut for and integer primary key with autoincrement is [c]AutoId[/c]
Please note that autoincrement sequences ALWAYS start from 1 for each entity individually, so you can use [c]PK == 0[/c] condition to check whether you are dealing with a new entity.
[b]Refer to Other Table[/b]
To create a reference to other entity:
Create a property that has the type of the other entity.
Mark it with [c]EntityProperty[/c] attribute with [c]ForeignKey[/c] property set to [c]true[/c].
In the case, the column with the type of the primary key of the other entity will be added to this table and the foreign key constraint will be created.
When [c]0..*[/c] relationship needs to be established, use [c]Nullable[/c] property of the attribute.
[b]Self-References Table[/b]
You can make self-reference to the entity in order to create a tree table.
Please note that:
The list reference must ALWAYS be nullable.
MySQL does NOT support tree queries, so using tree tables will exclude MySQL and MariaDb from the list of the DB that can be used in the application.
There are a few entity queries that can be created using [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.EntityQueries.EntityConnectionExtension]EntityConnectionExtension[/clink] extension class.

The methods of this class creates one of subclasses of [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.EntityQueries.EntityQuery]EntityQuery[/clink] classes. An entity query is a disposable object and must be disposed after use and before the connection to which the query belongs is disposed.
An entity queries can be executed using method [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.EntityQueries.EntityQuery.Execute.4MF]Execute[/clink].
Depending on the query it may have a parameter if the query operates on a single instance of the entity (e.g. insert, update or delete).
[b]Dropping and Creating an Individual Table[/b]
There are entity queries to drop and to create entity tables. See example below for both available ways to create an entity query:

Please note that the order, in which tables are dropped or created, matters. In case, say, you have an entity B that refers via a foreign key to an entity B, you have to drop B entity first and you have to create an entity A first.
[b]Using Entity Creation Controller[/b]

To simplify tables management, there are the class [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.EntityQueries.CreateEntityController]CreateEntityController[/clink] provided. The class loads all entities (or all entities filtered by a [i]scope[/i] specified) from the assembly and then orders them according relationships between assemblies. Then you can drop all entities or create all entities using just one call.
Of course, in case when you want to recreate all the entity tables, always drop entities first.

Please note that if you want your view automatically created and dropped, don't forget to provide metadata type in the [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Entities.EntityAttribute]EntityAttribute[/clink] that implements [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.Metadata.IViewCreationMetadata]IViewCreationMetadata[/clink] interface.
[b]Updating Entities[/b]

Often, during the application lifecycle, the database structure are changes to reflect new requirements and functionality. Not always full recreation of the database is the right answer, sometime updates are the option.
There are two typical ways on how the application can handle this situation:
Provide the update script that prepares the database for running a new version.

Use automatic update procedure, provided by [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.EntityQueries.CreateEntityController]CreateEntityController[/clink] class (see [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.EntityQueries.CreateEntityController.UpdateTables.Z93]Update()[/clink] method).
To update the database in the second way:
Create new entities and add new properties to the entities as usual.
Mark entities that won't be used anymore with [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Entities.ObsoleteEntityAttribute]ObsoleteEntity[/clink] attribute.
Mark entity properties that won't be used anymore with [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Entities.ObsoleteEntityPropertyAttribute]ObsoleteEntityProperty[/clink] attribute.

If additional actions is required when entity is changed (created, dropped) or property is changed (created, dropped) use the appropriate attribute that calls a static method that takes just one parameter - a connection. On drop actions are performed before the operation and on create are performed after. The attributes are:
[c]OnEntityCreate[/c]
[c]OnEntityDrop[/c]
[c]OnEntityPropertyCreate[/c]
[c]OnEntityPropertyDrop[/c]
Be aware of automatic updates limitations. The automatic update cannot:
Change type, name or attributes of an existing property.
Add or remove primary key of an existing entity.
Create a new non-nullable property. All newly introduced properties must be nullable.
There are [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.EntityQueries.ModifyEntityQuery]ModifyEntityQuery[/clink] that can be used to create, update or delete a single instance of the entity.
This query is the only query that requires the instance of the entity to be specified as a parameter of the [c]Execute[/c] method.

The using of the query is quite simple. Just get the query using [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.EntityQueries.EntityConnectionExtension]EntityConnectionExtension[/clink] extension class and call it for the instance. In case multiple entities must be handled, the same query may be reused multiple times.
If the primary key of the entity is integer autoincrement value, you can use universal approach that detects whether the entity needs to be updated or it needs to be inserted.
using (var query = entity.ID < 1 ? connection.GetInsertEntityQuery<Entity>() : connection.GetUpdateEntityQuery<Entity>()) query.Execute(entity);

article.title: Selecting Entities Count and Selecting All Entities

body.p: [b]Get Entities Count[/b]
To get number of entities use entity count query:
using (var query = connection.GetSelectEntitiesCountQuery<Entity>()) countOf = query.RowCount;
[b]Read All Entities[/b]
To read all entities (with looking up for the whole tree of foreign keys references):
If you don't want to read all entities at once but want to handle them one-by-one:
Select queries (and, by the way, multiple entity modification and deletion queries) can be applied to the filtered rows only.
To do it [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.EntityQueries.ConditionEntityQueryBase.Where.ty7]Where[/clink] property is used.
There are a lot of options how the resultset can be filtered. Let's discuss major options of filtering.
[b]Adding Simple Compare Conditions[/b]
The easiest way to filter is to simply compare a column value with a constant value.

The column is simply referenced by the property name. C# [c]nameof[/c] operation is very useful to specify the property names, so, the chance of mistype is reduced and no complex and boring replace operation would be needed if ever the property is renamed.
You can compare to all kind of equality comparison: is equal, is not equal, is greater, is greater or equal, is less, is less or equal.
[b]Is Null/Is Not Null Conditions[/b]
Is null/is not null conditions are even simpler as they do not require parameters:
[b]Add Conditions for Referenced Entities[/b]
The default entity query loads not only the main entity, but all referenced entities as well. These entities can also be used in the filter. All we need is to add the type of the entity referenced.
Let say we need to select entities that have referenced to dictionary entries that starts from the letter A.
[b]Subquery Conditions (IN)[/b]

Another query can be used as a part of the query reference. Here we are using the information that will be provided in further articles (on how to customize the resultset of an entity query), however, the examples below are quite simple and pretty much self-explaining.
Let say we need to select entities that have referenced to dictionary entries that starts from the letter A.

In this particular case this is just another (and less effective!) solution of the problem demonstrated in the previous example, however, this method can be used for custom queries which do not have references resolved or to more complex dictionary filtering, e.g. to select all dictionaries that have at least 10 entities referenced to them.
[b]Subquery Conditions With Cross-Reference[/b]

Sometimes subquery needs to be referenced to the parent query. Like in example below where we need to find all dictionary entries that do not have any entities related to them. The cross references may be established using the result returned by [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.EntityQueries.ConditionEntityQueryBase.GetReference]GetReference()[/clink] method.
[b]Put Conditions into Bracket and Joining With OR[/b]
Sometimes conditions needs to be joined by OR instead of AND (i.e. ANY of the conditions is true rather than ALL of the conditions are true)
Example below selects all rows where statistics is not set or is less than 100.

Sometimes, OR and AND conditions needs to be mixed. You must be aware that AND is always used first, before OR. In such cases the developer may want to enclose a set of conditions into a group in sake of predictable behavior and better readability of the query. The brackets needs to be used in this case. The principles of usage are example the same as using the brackets in the plain SQL or any other programming language.
The example below is equal to raw SQL [c]updateStatistics AND (statistics is NULL OR (statistics >= 20 AND statistics <= 180)[/c]
[b]Ordering Resultset[/b]

To order the resultset use [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.EntityQueries.SelectEntitiesQueryBase.AddOrderBy]AddOrderBy()[/clink] method of the query. The data can be ordered by any column of any entity involved into the query.
[b]Limiting Rows[/b]

To limit the number of rows received or to skip rows from the beginning of the query use [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.EntityQueries.SelectEntitiesQueryBase.Limit.yWG]Limit[/clink] and [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.EntityQueries.SelectEntitiesQueryBase.Skip.c83]Skip[/clink] properties of the query. The example below reads 20 entities starting at 100th entity.
Please be aware that some of database requires the resultset to be ordered to use limit and skip features.

article.title: Limit Which Columns are Selected (the filter method).

body.p: Sometimes, especially when the entity consists of large data such as clob or blob, it is reasonable to skip loading of this data when large amount of entities is received.
It is possible to do not load some columns while the entity is still loaded with all references resolved and is bound to the entity object using column filters.
The column filter is passed to the method of obtaining of the select query. The column filter simply lists the entity type and the column name to be skipped.
Skipped nullable properties will be filled with null values. Skipped non-nullable properties will be filled with default values of the property type.
In the example below, the entity property "Statistics" and the referenced property aren't loaded, however can be referenced in query filter.

The reference field may also be filtered, however this will affect only loading the data. The referenced entity will be included into the query and so it may be used in filters and order specification.

article.title: Limit Which Columns are Selected (the generic query method) and Aggregating Queries.

body.p: In case query need to produce custom resultset and custom entity query, a generic entity select query needs to be used. The generic entity select class is [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.EntityQueries.SelectEntitiesQueryBase]SelectEntitiesQueryBase[/clink]
[b]Defining Resultset[/b]
The generic entity query does not have any resultset defined when the object is created.
Use [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.EntityQueries.SelectEntitiesQueryBase.AddToResultset]AddToResultset()[/clink] method to specify which columns needs to be returned.
[b]Reading Resultset[/b]
There are two way to read the result of generic entity query.
First option is to read the resultset row-by-row and access each individual column using [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.EntityQueries.EntityQuery.GetValue]GetValue()[/clink] method.
The second option is to use dynamic objects. In this case it would be reasonable to explicitly define the names of the columns as they will be used as property names of the dynamic object.
[b]Aggregating the Data[/b]
The data in the generic query may be aggregated. Use [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Entities.AggFn]AggFn[/clink] function id while adding the data to the resultset.

When the data needs to be aggregated by some value, use [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.EntityQueries.SelectEntitiesQueryBase.AddGroupBy]AddGroupBy[/clink] method. Some database requires all columns from the resultset that aren't aggregate function to be included into group by clause. The framework automatically does this if at least one non-aggregated column exists in the resultset.
[b]Joins[/b]
Generic select by default has only one entity table involved, which was set when the query is created.
You can add more entities using [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.EntityQueries.SelectEntitiesQueryBase.AddEntity]AddEntity[/clink] method.
The framework automatically recognizes the type of join (inner, left or right) using primary and foreign key information in the entities.

In rare cases, especially when one entity is referenced in the other more than once, it requires to explicitly specify which column is used to join and which occurrence of the type in the query needs to be used in result set and/or the filter.

The LINQ extension class [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.EntityQueries.Linq.EntityQueryLinqExtension]EntityQueryLinqExtension[/clink] extends entity queries with ability to use LINQ expressions instead of types and names in resultset, filter and grouping specifications.

In most cases using of the LINQ expressions does not affect generated SQL quality and performance, however the expression compilation itself is slower than basic methods. The effect is not significant and can be ignored in applications that are not time or resource critical.
Sometimes the filters also may look simpler when specified via LINQ expression.
Let's look how we can alternatively specify the filtering condition (the entities will be the same for all examples in this article).
Let say we need to select entities that have value between 15 and 30 or that are lined to dictionary record with the name dict5.
First query in the example uses conventional approach to define the filter and the second query uses a LINQ expression for the same purpose.
LINQ expressions offers a few options which are not available for regular queries, such as:
It is possible to set custom join expression in [c]AddEntity[/c] method.
It is possible to add an expression into resultset.

Pay attention that where function is used, a function from class [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.EntityQueries.Linq.SqlFunction]SqlFunction[/clink] may only be used. The same class is also used to specify aggregate functions, when the operator does not exist in the C# language (e.g. like, in, exists) or when reference to the value in parent query needs to be used.

You can also have limited access to the entity using LINQ queries to a IQueriable collection of an entity. Use [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.EntityQueries.Linq.QueryableEntityProvider]QueryableEntityProvider[/clink] to get such collection.
Only the following methods are supported: from, group, order and select.

Below there are few examples of LINQ queries. Again it is positively advised against using true LINQ queries, especially in connection with the grouping operation because of poor performance of compiled SQL queries.
[b]SQL Queries on Entities[/b]
In some cases the job to be performed is more complex that supported by the current implementation of the entity framework. In this case you can fallback to plain SQL (or to use an SQL builder).
To get the SQL table related to the entity:
Find the entity descriptor using [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.EntityQueries.AllEntities]AllEntities[/clink] class.
Get the table descriptor using [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.EntityQueries.EntityDescriptor.TableDescriptor.I5A]TableDescriptor[/clink] property of the entity descriptor.
Note: You can always check what is the plain SQL select query by checking [c]SelectBuilder.SelectQueryBuilder.Query[/c] at your query AFTER your entity query is executed.
[b]SQL Queries on the result of an entity query[/b]

You can always apply a plain SQL select query on the results of entity select query. To add the entity select query result as a table to [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.QueryBuilder.SelectQueryBuilder]SelectQueryBuilder[/clink], use [c]SelectBuilder.SelectQueryBuilder.QueryTableDescriptor[/c] property of your query. (NOTE: You do not NEED to execute your query, it will be executed as a subquery of the plain SQL select query that you will construct).
Pay also attention at following features of the framework that aren't discussed above:

[clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.EntityGenericAccessor]EntityGenericAccessor[/clink] and [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.EntityGenericAccessor.GenericEntityAccessorWithAggregates]GenericEntityAccessorWithAggregates[/clink] classes that provides basic CRUD operations for entities.

[clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.EntityQueries.MultiUpdateEntityQuery]MultiUpdateEntityQuery[/clink] and [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.EntityQueries.MultiDeleteEntityQuery]MultiDeleteEntityQuery[/clink] queries.
Limited Bson and MongoDB support.

class.name: GenericEntityAccessorFilter
class.brief: Generic filter class for the generic accessor.

body.p: The whole idea of generic filter is that the developer just derives type-specific filter class from this one and just defines a set of properties making them up using FilterProperty attribute.
All filters are joined by AND

1) The filter properties types must be equal to entity property types in their nullable version, i.e. if the field is int, the filter type shall be int?. the null value will mean that filter is not used.
2) Should IsNull comparison be made, the filter property should be bool?. Null means that filter is inactive, true means IsNull and false means IsNotNull.

class.name: CreateEntityController
class.brief: The controller to create, drop or update entities automatically.

body.p: The method recognizes and handles the following cases:
Create table for the entity if the table does not exist.
Drop table for the entity if the table is attributed using [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Entities.ObsoleteEntityAttribute]ObsoleteEntityAttribute[/clink]
Add column for the entity if the column does not exist.
Drops column if the column attributed using [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Entities.ObsoleteEntityPropertyAttribute]ObsoleteEntityPropertyAttribute[/clink]

If you need to enrich the operation with the custom logic, you can use action (see [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.EntityQueries.EntityActionDelegate]EntityActionDelegate[/clink] ) or events [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.EntityQueries.CreateEntityController.OnAction.B4]CreateEntityController.OnAction[/clink] .

Note: Some connections do not support dropping of the columns. Check [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.SqlDbLanguageSpecifics.DropColumnSupported.vb5]SqlDbLanguageSpecifics.DropColumnSupported[/clink] flag to verify the connection. If connection does not support columns dropping, the columns will remain in the table.
Note: The controller does not recognize the situations when:
The type, name or other parameters of the column has changed.
The property deleted from the entity.
A new index is added via [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.Metadata.ICompositeIndexMetadata]ICompositeIndexMetadata[/clink]
View changed.
In case the change is too complex to be handled automatically, use the patches (see .

class.name: DynamicEntity
class.brief: The base class for dynamic entities.

body.p: Note: dynamic entities will not be discovered by default by [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Entities.EntityFinder]EntityFinder[/clink] . In order to enable discovery them in finder, call [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.EntityQueries.AllEntities.EnableDynamicEntityDiscoveryInEntityFinder.k26]AllEntities.EnableDynamicEntityDiscoveryInEntityFinder[/clink] method.

To implement a dynamic property, override [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.EntityQueries.DynamicEntity.EntityAttribute.SBF]DynamicEntity.EntityAttribute[/clink] property and [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.EntityQueries.DynamicEntity.InitializeProperties.dDF]DynamicEntity.InitializeProperties[/clink] method.

class.name: EntityActionDelegate
class.brief: The delegate type of the action called when entity is created.

body.p: The delegate prototype is:
public delegate void EntityActionDelegate(SqlDbConnection connection);
The action is invoked only when [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.EntityQueries.CreateEntityController]CreateEntityController[/clink] is used to create the entity.

To set the action use [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.EntityQueries.OnEntityCreateAttribute]OnEntityCreateAttribute[/clink] , [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.EntityQueries.OnEntityDropAttribute]OnEntityDropAttribute[/clink] , [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.EntityQueries.OnEntityPropertyCreateAttribute]OnEntityPropertyCreateAttribute[/clink] or [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.EntityQueries.OnEntityPropertyDropAttribute]OnEntityPropertyDropAttribute[/clink] .

The object instance must be disposed after use. Some databases requires the query to be disposed before the next query may be executed.

class.name: EntityQueryConditionBuilder
class.brief: The condition builder for WHERE and HAVING clauses of entity queries.

body.p: Each individual condition in the expression is build using [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.EntityQueries.SingleEntityQueryConditionBuilder]SingleEntityQueryConditionBuilder[/clink] .
Use [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.EntityQueries.EntityQueryConditionBuilderExtension]EntityQueryConditionBuilderExtension[/clink] to create an easier to read conditions.

member.name: Add
member.brief: Adds one condition and connects it to the rest of the expression using the specified logical operator.

body.p: Note: for the first condition only [c]Not[/c] is accounted. [c]And[/c] and [c]Or[/c] are ignored.

member.name: Add
member.brief: Adds a raw SQL expression.

member.name: AddGroup
member.brief: Adds a group of conditions and connects it to the already defined condition using the logical operator specified.

body.p: The method returns a disposable object. The condition is considered closed when the returned object is disposed.

member.name: AddGroup
member.brief: Adds a group of conditions and connects it to the already defined condition using the logical operator specified.

body.p: The condition is defined within the action specified.

class.name: EntityQueryConditionBuilderExtension
class.brief: The extension methods for the entity condition builder.

member.name: And
member.brief: Starts a new single condition and connects it to other conditions using logical and.

member.name: AndNot
member.brief: Starts a new single negative condition and connects it to other conditions using logical and.

member.name: Or
member.brief: Starts a new single condition and connects it to other conditions using logical or.

member.name: OrNot
member.brief: Starts a new single negative condition and connects it to other conditions using logical or.

member.name: Property
member.brief: Starts condition that compares a property and connects it to the other conditions using logical and.

member.name: Raw
member.brief: Starts raw condition connects it to the other conditions using logical and.

declaration.params: this [link=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.EntityQueries.EntityQueryConditionBuilder]EntityQueryConditionBuilder[/link] builder, string property, int occurrence = 0

member.name: Exists
member.brief: Starts checks whether the query returns a non-empty resultset connects it to the other conditions using logical and. The query is set by a query builder.

member.name: Exists
member.brief: Starts checks whether the query returns a non-empty resultset connects it to the other conditions using logical and. The query is set by an entity query.

member.name: NotExists
member.brief: Starts checks whether the query returns an empty resultset connects it to the other conditions using logical and. The query is set by a query builder.

member.name: NotExists
member.brief: Starts checks whether the query returns an empty resultset connects it to the other conditions using logical and. The query is set by an entity query.

member.name: And
member.brief: Starts a new group of conditions, define it using the actions specified and connect it to other conditions using logical and.

member.name: Or
member.brief: Starts a new group of conditions, define it using the actions specified and connect it to other conditions using logical or.

member.name: AndNot
member.brief: Starts a new group of conditions, define it using the actions specified, negate the condition and connect it to other conditions using logical and.

member.name: OrNot
member.brief: Starts a new group of conditions, define it using the actions specified, negate the condition and connect it to other conditions using logical or.

Use [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.EntityQueries.DynamicEntityProperty]DynamicEntityProperty[/clink] as a dynamic implementation.

class.name: IEntityDisoverer
class.brief: The interface for entity discoverer.

body.p: Implement this interface and add it using [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.EntityQueries.AllEntities.AddDiscoverer.L0A]AllEntities.AddDiscoverer[/clink] to enable another way to discover entities.

If you implement this interface, implement also [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Entities.IEntityProbe]IEntityProbe[/clink] interface to enable finding the entities by [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Entities.EntityFinder]EntityFinder[/clink] .

class.name: IEntitySerializationCallback
class.brief: The serialization callback interface.

body.p: If the entity implements this interface, this method is called every time before the entity is saved to the database or after the entity is restored.

member.brief: The entity is about to be saved.

member.name: AfterDeserealization
member.brief: The entity has just been restored.

class.name: SqlFunction
class.brief: The list of SQL functions to be used in the query-related expression.

body.p: Using this function forces the compiler to execute the function on the server side. For example, is [c]string.Upper()[/c] is used and the arguments can be calculated locally, it will be calculated locally. But if [c]SqlFunction.Upper()[/c] is used the function will always be executed on the server side.

class.name: OnEntityCreateAttribute
class.brief: The attribute to set the action to be called when entity is created.

body.p: The action will be called only if the entity is created using
[clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.EntityQueries.CreateEntityController]CreateEntityController[/clink] .
The type that consists of action method.
The action method name. The method should match either [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.EntityQueries.EntityActionDelegate]EntityActionDelegate[/clink]

class.name: OnEntityDropAttribute
class.brief: The attribute to set the action to be called when entity is dropped.

body.p: The action will be called only if the entity is dropped using
[clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.EntityQueries.CreateEntityController]CreateEntityController[/clink] .

class.name: SelectEntitiesQuery

body.p: Use [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.EntityQueries.EntityConnectionExtension.GetSelectEntitiesQuery.SH4]EntityConnectionExtension.GetSelectEntitiesQuery[/clink] to get an instance of this query.

In cause you don't need complete tree/all columns, use [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.EntityQueries.SelectEntitiesQueryBase]SelectEntitiesQueryBase[/clink] to minimize DB load and traffic between the application and database.
The object instance must be disposed after use. Some databases requires the query to be disposed before the next query may be executed.

class.name: ConditionBuilder
class.brief: The build of the condition.

body.p: The most convenient way to use the condition builder is to add [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.QueryBuilder.SingleConditionBuilder]SingleConditionBuilder[/clink] for each individual condition using [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.QueryBuilder.ConditionBuilder.Add.IcA]ConditionBuilder.Add[/clink] or [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.QueryBuilder.ConditionBuilder.AddGroup.qJ3]ConditionBuilder.AddGroup[/clink] methods.
The rest of methods are designed for EF infrastructure or may be used to construct complex queries that aren't supported by EF.
Use also [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.QueryBuilder.ConditionBuilderExtension]ConditionBuilderExtension[/clink] to easier and more readable definition of the conditions.
The condition builder is used to build [c]where[/c], [c]on[/c] and [c]having[/c] clauses of SQL queries.

member.name: Add
member.brief: Adds raw SQL expression and connects it to previous condition with the specified logical operator.

member.name: Add
member.brief: Adds an expression defined by two raw SQL argument and comparison operation connects it to previous condition with the specified logical operator.

declaration.name: Add
declaration.params: [link=Gehtsoft.EF.Entities.LogOp]LogOp[/link] logOp, string leftSide, [link=Gehtsoft.EF.Entities.CmpOp]CmpOp[/link] cmpOp, string rightSide

member.name: Add
member.brief: Adds an expression defined by two raw SQL argument and comparison operation connects it to previous condition with the logical and operator.

member.name: AddGroup
member.brief: Adds a group of conditions enclosed into brackets and connects it with the previous expression using the specified logical operator.

body.p: The group of conditions is considered finished when the object returned is disposed.

member.name: Add
member.brief: Adds a single condition and connects it using the specified logical operator.

member.name: Add
member.brief: Adds a group of conditions enclosed into brackets and connects it with the previous expression using the specified logical operator.

body.p: Pass an action to define the content of the condition group.

member.brief: Adds a complex condition in brackets and connects it to the previous condition using logical or.

member.name: Property

member.name: Property
member.brief: Starts a new single condition, sets the a column of the first table in the query as the first argument and connect it to the previous condition with logical and.

member.name: Property

member.name: Property

member.name: Parameter
member.brief: Starts a new single condition, sets the parameter specified as the first argument and connect it to the previous condition with logical and.

member.name: Reference
member.brief: Starts a new single condition, sets column reference as the first argument and connect it to the previous condition with logical and.

member.name: Exists
member.brief: Starts a new single condition, connect it to the previous expression using logical and, and set the operator to exists.

member.name: NotExists
member.brief: Starts a new single condition, connect it to the previous expression using logical and, and set the operator to not exists.

member.name: Raw
member.brief: Starts a new single condition, sets the raw SQL expression as the first argument and connect it to the previous condition with logical and.

body.p: If auto-connection flag is set to [c]true[/c] the table builder will try to connect the entity to the first entity which is related to this entity by having a foreign key to this entity or by being reference via foreign key from this entity.
If the automatic connection does not product the desired result, please use disable auto-connection
[clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.QueryBuilder.QueryBuilderEntity.On.p81]QueryBuilderEntity.On[/clink] property of the returned object to configure the join condition.

The flag indicating whether the table needs to be auto-connected to the existing tables

class.name: SingleConditionBuilder
class.brief: Builder for a single condition.

body.p: The builder for a single condition is returned by [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.QueryBuilder.ConditionBuilder]ConditionBuilder[/clink] .
Define a condition as a following:
Set the left side
Set the operation
Set the right side
query.Where.Property(table["column"]).Is(CmpOp.Eq).Parameter("c1");

Use this class to define one comparison or check within a complex condition. See [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.QueryBuilder.SingleConditionBuilderExtension]SingleConditionBuilderExtension[/clink] to easier and more readable definition of the condition.

class.name: UnionQueryBuilder
class.brief: The query builder for [c]UNION[/c] query.

body.p: Use [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.SqlDbConnection.GetUnionQueryBuilder.ATF]SqlDbConnection.GetUnionQueryBuilder[/clink] to get an instance of this object.

member.name: QueryTableDescriptor
member.brief: Returns the table descriptor of the query resultset.

member.name: AddQuery
member.brief: Adds a query to the union.

body.p: The query must:
Have the same number of columns
The columns should be name the same way
The columns should have the same data type
Order should not be defined.
The query to add
A flag indicating whether the union should take only distinct record ([c]true[/c]) or all record, including duplicates (`false)

member.name: AddOrderBy
member.brief: Adds order by column for the union.

body.p: The column must be a column of the union's resultset (see [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.QueryBuilder.UnionQueryBuilder.QueryTableDescriptor.Xw5]UnionQueryBuilder.QueryTableDescriptor[/clink] )

body.p: The parameter name to set the ID will be the same as the primary key column name.

class.name: SqlDbTransaction
class.brief: The database transaction.

body.p: Use [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.SqlDbConnection.BeginTransaction.CC1]SqlDbConnection.BeginTransaction[/clink] to create a transaction.
The transaction is considered ended when the object is disposed. The transaction should be committed explicitly, otherwise the driver will roll it back at the disposal.

member.brief: Adds a "greater than or equals to" comparison with the value specified to the condition.

member.name: Ls
member.brief: Adds a "less than" comparison with the value specified to the condition.

member.name: Le
member.brief: Adds a "less than or equals to" comparison with the value specified to the condition.

member.name: Like
member.brief: Adds an "is like" comparison with the value specified to the condition.

class.name: IContextCount
class.brief: The context count query.

member.name: GetCount
member.brief: Returns the number of the rows counted.

member.name: GetCountAsync
member.brief: Returns the number of the rows counted asynchronously.

class.name: IContextFilter
class.brief: The filter for all queries with the condition.

body.p: The filter is the sequence of the [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Entities.Context.IContextFilterCondition]IContextFilterCondition[/clink] joined by Logical And or Logical Or operators.
Use [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Entities.Context.EntityFilterBuilderExtension]EntityFilterBuilderExtension[/clink] for more readable definition of the filters.

member.name: AddGroup
member.brief: Adds a group of the conditions (conditions enclosed into brackets)

body.p: connected to the previously set conditions by the operator specified.
The condition considered finished when the returns object is disposed.

member.name: Add
member.brief: Adds a condition.

class.name: IContextFilterCondition
class.brief: A single condition inside the filter expression.

body.p: Example:
condition.Property("a").Is(CmpOp.Eq).Value(1); condition.Property("a").Is(CmpOp.Le).Property(b);
See also [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Entities.Context.IContextFilter]IContextFilter[/clink] .
Use [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Entities.Context.EntityFilterConditionExtension]EntityFilterConditionExtension[/clink] for more readable definition of the conditions.

member.name: Property
member.brief: Adds a property to the condition.

member.name: Is
member.brief: Sets the operation.

member.name: Value
member.brief: Adds the value of the condition.

class.name: IContextOrder
class.brief: The order definition.

member.name: Add
member.brief: Adds an column to the order definition.

class.name: IContextQueryWithCondition
class.brief: The query that has a condition (filter).

member.name: Where
member.brief: The condition.

class.name: IContextSelect
class.brief: The query that selects the entities according the filter specified.

member.name: Order
member.brief: The order definition.

member.name: Take
member.brief: The number of the records to take.

body.p: If value is not specified, all records will be read.

member.name: Skip
member.brief: The number of records to skip.

member.name: ReadOne
member.brief: Reads one entity.

body.p: If there is no more entities, the method returns [c]null[/c].

member.name: ReadOneAsync
member.brief: Reads one entity asynchronously.

body.p: If there is no more entities, the task will return [c]null[/c].

class.name: IEntityContext
class.brief: The interface to the context of the entities.

body.p: You can use this interface as a simplified way to access entities. This interface is independent from the specifics of the entity storage implementation (e.g. SQL or NoSQL).

member.brief: Returns the list of the tables.

member.name: DropEntity
member.brief: Returns a query to drop the table associated with the entity.

member.name: CreateEntity
member.brief: Returns a query to create a table associated with the entity.

member.name: InsertEntity
member.brief: Returns a query to insert an entity to the storage.

body.p: The flag indicating whether automatic (e.g. auto-insert) keys should be automatically created.

member.name: UpdateEntity
member.brief: Returns a query to update the entity in the storage.

member.name: DeleteEntity
member.brief: Returns a query to delete one entity from the storage.

member.name: DeleteMultiple
member.brief: Returns a query to delete multiple entities from the storage.

member.name: Select
member.brief: Returns a query to select entities from the storage.

member.name: Count
member.brief: Returns a query to count the number of the entities in the storage.

member.name: BeginTransaction
member.brief: Begins the transaction operation.

class.name: IEntityContextTransaction
class.brief: The transaction with entity context.

body.p: The transaction is a group operation that will be either executed all or not executed at all.
Please note that not all context (especially non-SQL) will support transactions.

member.name: Commit
member.brief: Commits the transaction.

body.p: The transaction will be considered failed unless it is explicitly commited.

member.name: Rollback
member.brief: Rollsback transaction.

member.name: Scope
member.brief: The name of the entity scope.

member.name: Table
member.brief: The name of the table name.

body.p: If no name is set, the entity table name will be created according applicable [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Entities.EntityAttribute.NamingPolicy.5nF]EntityAttribute.NamingPolicy[/clink] .

member.name: View
member.brief: The flag indicating whether the entity is associated with a view.

body.p: If the flag is [c]true[/c] the entity is a view.
If the flag is [c]false[/c] (default) the entity is a table.

member.name: Metadata
member.brief: The optional metadata type.

body.p: The metadata object may be used to provide additional information, for example:
The metadata implements [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.Metadata.ICompositeIndexMetadata]ICompositeIndexMetadata[/clink] to provide information about composite indexes.
The metadata implements [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.Metadata.IViewCreationMetadata]IViewCreationMetadata[/clink] to provide select query for automatic view creation.

member.name: NamingPolicy
member.brief: The naming policy.

body.p: If no naming policy is explicitly set, the naming policy associated with the scope or default naming policy will be used.
The default naming policy is defined via [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.EntityQueries.AllEntities.NamingPolicy.R38]AllEntities.NamingPolicy[/clink].

body.p: The metadata object is used to provide additional information about the entity. To provide that information the metadata should implement appropriate interface, for example [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.Metadata.ICompositeIndexMetadata]ICompositeIndexMetadata[/clink] or [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Db.SqlDb.Metadata.IViewCreationMetadata]IViewCreationMetadata[/clink].

class.name: EntityNameConvertor
class.brief: The name converter used for entity policies

member.name: ConvertTableName
member.brief: Converts the table name according the policy specified.

member.name: ConvertName
member.brief: Converts a column name according the policy specified.

class.name: EntityNamingPolicy
class.brief: The naming policies for the table and column names.

body.p: The policy defines how the name will be converted.
Use AllEntities.NamingPolicy to set the naming policy.

member.name: Default
member.brief: Default naming policy.

body.p: The default policy is to use the names as is.
The last word in the entity name is considered an English noun and converted to proper plural form, e.g. the entity class [c]Apple[/c] will have table name [c]Apples[/c].

member.name: BackwardCompatibility
member.brief: The naming policy compatible with pre 1.2 version of EF library.

body.p: In this naming policy the table name is an entity name as is and the column name is a lower case entity name.

member.name: AsIs
member.brief: The table and column names are used as is.

member.name: LowerCase
member.brief: The table and column names are converted to lowercase.

member.name: UpperCase
member.brief: The table and column names are converted to uppercase.

member.name: LowerFirstCharacter
member.brief: The table and column names have first character converted to lower case and the rest used as is.

member.name: UpperFirstCharacter
member.brief: The table and column names have first character converted to upper case and the rest used as is.

member.name: LowerCaseWithUnderscores
member.brief: The table and column names are converted to lower case. In all locations where there the case changed, an underscore will be added.

body.p: For example the name [c]LongComplexName[/c] will be changed to [c]long_complex_name[/c].

member.name: UpperCaseWithUnderscopes
member.brief: The table and column names are converted to lower case. In all locations where there the case changed, an underscore will be added.

body.p: For example the name [c]LongComplexName[/c] will be changed to [c]LONG_COMPLEX_NAME[/c].

body.p: If the value is not set, the database type will be calculate automatically using the rules for each database.

member.name: Size
member.brief: The maximum size of the column.

body.p: If is important to set the size to string, binary and numeric data types to avoid bloating the tables.

member.name: Precision

body.p: for real numbers.

body.p: is a primary key.

body.p: is auto increment value.

member.name: ForeignKey
member.brief: The value indicating that the property is

body.p: a reference to another entity.

member.name: Sorted
member.brief: The flag forcing creating an index for the column.

member.name: Unique
member.brief: The flag indicating that the value is unique.

member.name: Nullable
member.brief: The flag indicating that the value can be a [c]null[/c] value.

body.p: NOTE: If type is automatically defined and the property value has a nullable type, the column will be created with a nullable value.

member.name: IgnoreRead
member.brief: The flag to prevent automatic including the entity

body.p: property to "real all" queries.

member.name: DefaultValue
member.brief: The default value.

body.p: Only primitive types, such as string or numbers are supported.

class.brief: Internal class, Do not use it directly.

class.name: PrimaryKeyAttribute
class.brief: The attribute to mark a property as a primary key.

body.p: You can use it instead of [clink=Gehtsoft.EF.Entities.EntityPropertyAttribute]EntityPropertyAttribute[/clink] .

class.name: SortDir
class.brief: Sorting direction.

class.name: TableJoinType
class.brief: Kinds of table joins

body.p: No-join means returning of all possible combinations of the records from left and right tables.
In inner join only records that exactly matches are selected

member.name: Left
member.brief: Left Outer

body.p: In left outer join all records that exactly matches, plus records from the first table that have no matches in right table are selected.

member.name: Right
member.brief: Right Outer

body.p: In right outer join all records that exactly matches, plus records from the second table that have no matches in left table are select.

member.name: Outer
member.brief: Full Outer

body.p: In full outer join all matches, and all records without match from left and right tables are returned.
